5656.history: Assigned USN penant DE 85 but not named. HMS Dakins (A/Lt. Michael Geoffrey Henderson Arbuthnot, RNVR) was damaged beyond feasible repair when mined on 25 December 1944 north-west of Ostend, Belgium in position 51º25'N, 02º44'E. Managed to limp back to Harwich. Served as depot ship. Scrapped on 9 January 1947 in the Netherlands.
commanders: T/Lt. Michael Geoffrey Henderson Arbuthnot, RNVR (23 Nov 1943 - early 1945)

5657.history: Assigned USN penant DE 86 but not named. Returned to the USN on 4 March 1946. Scrapped.

T/Lt.Cdr. Frederick Brock, RCNVR (20 Dec 1944 - 18 Mar 1945)
A/Lt.Cdr. Edward Lewis Cook, DSC, RN (18 Mar 1945 - early 1946)

5658.history: Assigned USN penant DE 87 but not named. HMS Ekins (Lt. A.R. Tandevin, RNVR) was severely damaged by a mine in the North Sea some 13 miles north-west of Ostend, Belgium on 16 April 1945, declared a total loss. HMS Ekins is not listed as active unit in the October 1945 Navy List Scrapped in the Netherlands.
commanders: T/Lt. George Graham Bonner-Davis, RNVR (Nov 1943 - 16 Oct 1944)
Lt. Anthony Richard Taudevin, DSC, RNVR (16 Oct 1944 - mid 1945)
T/Lt. Robert John Slater, RNVR (mid 1945 - mid 1945)

5659.history: Assigned USN penant DE 88 but not named. HMS Fitzroy is not listed as active unit in the October 1945 Navy List Returned to the USN on 6 January 1946. Scrapped. Battle Honours include; Battle of Atlantic - Arctic Convoys & Normandy Landings.
commanders: Cdr. Colin Henry Campbell, DSC, RN (16 Oct 1943 - Jan 1944)
Lt. Charles Duncan Campbell McNeil, RNVR (Jan 1944 - 17 Apr 1944)

Lt.Cdr. Orme Gordon Stuart, RCNVR (early 1945 - mid 1945)

5660.history: Assigned USN penant DE 89 but not named. On 27 April 1945 HMS Redmill (Lt. John Richard Alured Denne, RN) was torpedoed by the German submarine U-1105 west of Ireland in position 54º23'N, 10º36'W. She was hit by a Gnat torpedo and had 60 feet of her stern missing. She was towed to Lisahally and declared a total loss. Returned to the US Navy on 20 January 1947 and scrapped. Hit by U-boatA total loss on 27 Apr 1945 by U-1105 (Schwarz).
commanders: Lt. John Richard Alured Denne, RN (3 Dec 1943 - mid 1945)

5662.history: Assigned USN penant DE 91 but not named. HMS Halsted (Cdr. John Rowe Westmacott, RN) was torpedoed in the English Channel by a German Motor Torpedo Boat (E-boat) on 11 June 1944. The bow was blown off. Declared a total loss and used for spare parts. Scrapped starting on 28 March 1947.
commanders: Cdr. John Rowe Westmacott, RN (25 Feb 1944 - 3 Jul 1944)
T/Lt. William Richard Fowler, RNVR (3 Jul 1944 - late 1944)

5668.history: Assigned USN penant DE 516 but not named. HMS Lawford (A/Lt.Cdr. Malcolm Guy Morris, DSC, RNR) was sunk on 8 June, 1944 off Normandy by German aircraft while serving as headquarters ship.The wreck lies in 21 meter of water in position 49º25'43"N, 00º23'47"W.
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. Sydney Ayles, RNR (3 Nov 1943 - 14 Jan 1944)
Lt. Malcolm Guy Morris, DSC, RD, RNR (14 Jan 1944 - 8 Jun 1944)

5671.history: Assigned USN penant DE 519 but not named. Transferred to Britain under the Lend-Lease Programme. First named HMS Lindsay but as it became clear that there was a Canadian corvette called HMCS Lindsay she was renamed HMS Pasley shortly after being christened. Returned to the USN on 20 March 1946 and broken up for scrap.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. James Hutchinson Wright, RD, RNR (15 Nov 1943 - Jun 1945)

5672.history: Assigned USN penant DE 520 but not named. HMS Loring is not listed as active unit in the October 1945 Navy List Returned to the USN on 7 January 1947. Scrapped.

Lt. John Augustus Ogilvy, RN (mid 1944 - mid 1945)
Lt. Anthony Michael Edlin, RNVR (mid 1945 - mid 1945)

Lt. Peter James Hill Hoare, RN (Feb 1944 - mid 1945)

5674.history: Assigned USN pennant DE 522 but not named. HMS Moorsom is not listed as active unit in the July 1945 Navy List Returned to the US Navy on 25 October 1945. Scrapped.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. John Parker Stewart, DSC, RD, RNR (10 Dec 1943 - )

5675.history: Assigned USN penant DE 523 but not named. On 26 January 1945, HMS Manners (A/Cdr. John Valentine Waterhouse, DSO, RN) was hit by one torpedo from U-1051 some 20 miles from Skerries, Isle of Man. The U-boat was sunk by the counter-attack of several escorts from the 4th and 5th Escort Group. The frigate broke in two after the hit, the stern sank with the loss of four officers and 39 ratings, while 15 others were injured. The forepart of the vessel was towed into Barrow in Furness and was declared a total loss. HMS Manners is not listed as active unit in the April 1945 Navy List On 12 December 1946 the wreck was sold to a Greek ship breaker and scrapped 1947 in Piraeus, Greece. Hit by U-boatA total loss on 26 Jan 1945 by U-1051 (Holleben).
commanders: Lt. Denis Jermain, DSC, RN (16 Dec 1943 - 27 Nov 1944)
A/Cdr. John Valentine Waterhouse, DSO, RN (27 Nov 1944 - Jan 1945 ?)

5676.history: Assigned USN penant DE 524 but not named. Returned to the USN on 25 February 1946. Scrapped. Hit by U-boatDamaged on 2 Nov 1944 by U-295 (Wieboldt).

5680.history: Assigned USN penant DE 566 but not named. HMS Trollope (Lt.Cdr. Harry Westacott, RN) was torpedoed and damaged off Cap d'Antifer on 6 July, 1944. A total loss. Sold for scrap in May 1951.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Harry Westacott, RN (10 Jan 1944 - 6 Jul 1944 (+))

5685.history: Assigned USN penant DE 571 but not named. HMS Whitaker (Lt. Glynn Percy Watkin Edwards, RN) was torpedoed off Malin Head in position 55º30'N, 07º39'W by the German submarine U-483, a total loss, towed to port and returned to the USN in March 1945. Scrapped Hit by U-boatA total loss on 1 Nov 1944 by U-483 (Morstein).

5687.history: Assigned USN penant DE 96 but had not been named. Returned to the USN on 20 March 1946. Stricken on 17 April 1946 and scrapped.

5689.history: Assigned USN penant DE 574 but not named. Returned to the USN on 13 March 1946. Scrapped. Hotham
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. Sydney Ayles, RNR (Mar 1944 - 8 Sep 1945)

5690.history: Oriskany was renamed Wasp (ii) on 26 September 1942. Decommissioned 17 February 1947. Recommissioned 28 September 1951. Decommissioned and stricken 1 July 1972. Sold 21 May 1973 and broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/R.Adm. Clifton Albert Frederick Sprague, USN (24 Nov 1943 - 22 Jul 1944)
T/Capt. Oscar Arthur Weller, USN (22 Jul 1944 - 7 Jun 1945)
T/Capt. Wendell Gray Switzer, USN (7 Jun 1945 - 4 Mar 1946)

5691.built_by: Hall, Russell & Co. Ltd. (Aberdeen, Scotland) : Thornycroft (Southampton, U.K.)
5691.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 20 June 1945. Bought by Royal Danish Navy on 27 november 1945 and renamed Niels Ebbesen. Scrapped 1963 at Odense, Denmark.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Charles Patrick Balfry, RCNR (11 Mar 1944 - 26 May 1945)
J H Corbett, RCNVR (27 May 1945 - 20 Jun 1945)

5692.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 30 May 1945. Scrapped in June 1953.
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. William Roland Stacey, RCNR (29 Jan 1944 - 15 Mar 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Edward Middlemas More, RCNR (16 Mar 1944 - 30 May 1945)

5693.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 23 April 1945. Scrapped 14 May 1966.

5694.built_by: Charles Hill & Sons Ltd. (Bristol, U.K.) : Bellis & Morcom
5694.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 11 June 1945. Bought by Royal Danish navy and renamed Holger Danske. Scrapped in 1960 at Odense, Denmark.

5695.built_by: Smith's Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
5695.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 11 June 1945. Scrapped in August 1955.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Eric Rigby Shaw, RCNR (28 Mar 1944 - 11 Jun 1945)

5696.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)
5696.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 11 June 1945. Scrapped 9 July 1957.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Allan Brown Taylor, RCNR (14 May 1944 - 20 Nov 1944)
A/Lt.Cdr. Aubrey Alvin Randle Dykes, RCNR (21 Nov 1944 - 11 Jun 1945)

5697.built_by: Smith's Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
5697.history: HMCS Teme (T/Lt Dennis Pearce Harvey, RCNVR) was torpedoed and damaged by the German submarine U-315 on 29 March 1945 off Lands End, in postion 50º07'N, 05º45'W, and declared a Constructive Total Loss (CTL). Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 4 May 1945. Sold to be broken up for scrap 8 December 1945. Hit by U-boatA total loss on 29 Mar 1945 by U-315 (Zoller).
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Douglas George Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR (23 Feb 1944 - 20 Feb 1945)
T/Lt. Dennis Pearce Harvey, RCNVR (21 Feb 1945 - 3 May 1945)

5698.history: Transferred to the Polish Navy on 4 October 1944. She was returned to the Royal Navy on 28 September 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 26 January 1948. Arrived to be scrapped at T.W. Ward at Barrow on 27 March 1948.
commanders: Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) Stanislaw Tytus Dzienisiewicz, ORP (4 Oct 1944 - 26 Jul 1945)
Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) Romuald Nalecz-Tyminski, ORP (27 Jul 1945 - 28 Sep 1946)

5699.history: This ship was manned by men from the Polish navy from 15 January 1943. She was damaged 8th July 1944 (37 killed and 14 injured) off the Normandy beaches and expended as breakwater.
commanders: Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) Aleksander Hulewicz, ORP (15 May 1943 - 15 Jul 1943)
Kmdr. (Capt.) Eugeniusz Jozef Stanislaw Plawski, ORP (15 Jul 1943 - 12 Jan 1944)
Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) Stanislaw Tytus Dzienisiewicz, ORP (12 Jan 1944 - 15 Jul 1944)

5700.built_by: Chanteliers et Ateliers Augustin Normand (Le Havre, France)
5700.history: Gryf means griffen in Polish. Gryf (Komandur Podporucznik Stefan Kwiatkowski) was sunk at Hel by German Stuka dive bombers (along with destroyer Wicher and several smaller ships). 21 of the crew were killed.
commanders: Kmdr.ppor. (Cdr.) Stanislaw Tytus Dzienisiewicz, ORP (27 Feb 1938 - 16 Sep 1938)
Kmdr por. Roman Wiktor Stankiewicz, ORP (16 Oct 1938 - 1 Apr 1939)
Kmdr ppor. Stefan Kwiatkowski, ORP (1 Apr 1939 - 1 Sep 1939 (+))
Kpt. Mar. Wiktor Lomidze, ORP (1 Sep 1939 - 2 Sep 1939)
Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) Stanislaw Hryniewiecki, ORP (2 Sep 1939 - 3 Sep 1939)

5701.built_by: New York Shipbuilding Corp. (Camden, New Jersey, U.S.A.)
5701.history: In September 1947 she was about 84% completed and then it was planned to convert her into the first guided missile ship. She was designated a large command ship CBC-1 on 26 Feb, 1952 when another conversion was planned. Cancelled and struck from the navy list on 9 June, 1958. Sold for scrap in 1959.

5702.history: Decommissioned 3 February 1947. Stricken 1 March 1959. Sold 14 August 1959 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: Capt. Thaddeus Austin Thomson, Jr., USN (16 Feb 1939 - 27 Dec 1941)
Capt. James Thomas Alexander, USN (27 Dec 1941 - 20 Feb 1942)
Capt. Harry Wilbur Hill, USN (20 Feb 1942 - 3 Sep 1942 (1))
T/R.Adm. Francis Stuart Low, USN (3 Sep 1942 - 10 Mar 1943 (1))
Capt. John Joseph Mahoney, USN (10 Mar 1943 - 11 Apr 1944 (1))
Capt. Douglas Ancrum Spencer, USN (11 Apr 1944 - 4 Jun 1945 (1))
T/Capt. Charles Joseph Rend, USN (4 Jun 1945 - Dec 1945)
T/Capt. William Hibbs, USN (Dec 1945 - Apr 1946 (1))

5703.built_by: Federal Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. (Newark, New Jersey, U.S.A.)
5703.history: Decommissioned in 1970. Stricken 1 July 1973. Sold 6 November 1974 and broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Thomas Henry Copeman, USN (3 May 1945 - 16 Nov 1945)

5704.built_by: Federal Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. (Newark, New Jersey, U.S.A.)
5704.history: Reclassified as DDR-711 on 18 July 1952. Reverted back to DD-711 on 15 March 1963. Decommissioned 31 August 1972 and loaned to Spain being renamed Churruca (D61). Stricken 2 June 1975. Sold to Spain 17 May 1978. Churruca was stricken by Spain 15 September 1989. Used for target practice and sunk by the Spanish Navy on 12 December 1991. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: T/Cdr. William Veazie Pratt, 2nd, USN (8 Jun 1945 - 6 Sep 1947)

5705.built_by: Federal Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. (Newark, New Jersey, U.S.A.)
5705.history: Reclassified as DDG-1 on 1 December 1955. Reclassified to DDG-712 on 30 December 1956. Reclassified back to DDG-1 on 23 May 1957. Reverted back to DD-712 on 1 October 1962. Decommissioned at an unknown date. Stricken 22 October 1969. Sunk as a target off the coast of Virginia 11 June 1970.

5744.history: Decommissioned in 1982. Transferred to Turkey 22 February 1982 being renamed Alcitepe. Alcitepe was removed from service in the Turkish Navy in 1998.

5745.history: USS Timmerman was constructed as an experimental, light weight, advanced design destroyer to test and evaluate, under operating conditions, advanced design experimental engineering equipment. As a unit of the Operational Development Force, 1st Naval District, Boston, she tested her new propulsion system for the next four years. On 11 January 1954, her designation was changed to AG-152, a miscellaneous auxiliary ship. The ship was decommissioned at Boston on 27 July 1956. She was moved to Philadelphia in September and assigned to the Reserve Fleet. In early 1958 Timmerman was declared unfit for further service and struck from the Navy list on 4 April 1958. On 21 April 1959, she was sold to the Boston Metals Co., Baltimore, Maryland., and scrapped.

5746.history: Reclassified as DDR-829 on March 1949. Reverted back to DD-829 on 1 April 1964. Decommissioned at an unknown date. Stricken 1 October 1979. Transferred to Greece 2 August 1980 and cannibalized for spare parts.

5748.history: The USS Goddrich was named in honor of Rear Admiral Casper F. Goodrich, USN, former Commander-in- Chief Pacific Squardron and Commandant, first Naval District who died in 1925 and his son, Lieutenant Casper Goodrich, USN, who lost his life in a gun turret explosion on board the battleship, USS Georgia in 1907. It was the first U.S.Navy ship named in honor of a father and son. She commenced her career by transiting the Panama Canel to operate with the Fifth and Seventh fleets in Japanese and Korean waters. After World War Two, the Goodrich assisted in the sinking of 24 Japanese submarines, including the I-58 which was responsible for sinking the USS Indianapolis. In January 1947, she returned to the Atlantic Fleet as a unit of Destroyer Squardon Eight and Fourteen, homeported at Newport, Rhode Island. In June 1952, she escorted the luxury liner SS United States into New York Harbor prior to the liner's maiden voyage across the Atlantic. In June 1953, she entered the Boston Naval Shipyard and was converted to a radar picket destroyer. The Goodrich moved her homeport to Mayport, Florida, Destroyer Squardron Eight, in June 1959 and in January 1960 she entered Norfolk Naval Shipyard for an eight month complete Fleet Rehabilitation and Modernization Overhaul. There she received a complete modernization from stem to stern and had the latest equipment installed. Since 1948, the Goodrich has deployed to the Mediterranean fifteen times to serve with the Sixth fleet. In connection with these deployments, she had participated in many NATO exercises and operations, the first Red Sea Patrol in 1956, the Lebanese Operation in 1958, the Cyprus Crisis in March 1964 and again in November 1967, and Special Operations in the Black Sea in November, 1967. In February, 1962, the Goodrich was on recovery station as part of Project Mercury and the resulting successful orbital flight of Lieutenant John H. Glenn. During her years of commission, the USS Goodrich has earned the American Theater Medal, the Asiatic Pacific Theater Medal, the World War II Victory Medal, the Navy Occupation Medal, the China Service Medal and the National Defence Service Medal. the Goodrich also earned Anti-Air-Warfare Awards and three Battle Efficiency "E"s. The last award earned by the Goodrich was selection as the Sixth Fleet Ship of the Year for 1968 and nomination for "Our Navy" Magazine's Ship of the Year. The Goodrich returned to Mayport, Florida on 30 January 1969 upon completion of her fifteenth and final deployment to the Mediterranean. She conducted fleet operations until 22 August 1969 when she was notified of her scheduled decommissioning. On 16 September 1969 she got underway on her last cruise, arriving at the Naval Inactive Ship Maintenance Facility, Orange, Texas on 19 September 1969. The Goodrich was placed "in commission-in-reserve" status on 22 September 1969, and was decommissioned on 12 December 1969. On 12 September 1977 she was sold to the New York Scrap and Alloy Company where she was eventually scrapped.
commanders: T/Cdr. Dale Roderick Frakes, USN (21 Apr 1945 - 7 Sep 1946)

5749.history: Reclassified as DDR-832 on 18 March 1949. Reverted back to DD-832 on 1 April 1964. Decommissioned at an unknown date. Stricken 31 March 1973.
